framing: OncoEvidence: Counterfactual Evidence-Triage for Mechanism-Guided Cancer Drug Repurposing -- mechanism-novelty triage.

label_definitions.known_mechanism: drug->gene MOA curated in DrugMechDB (textbook fact)
label_definitions.known_drug_new_cancer: established oncology drug, plausible MOA path, new cancer
label_definitions.new_mechanism: specific MOA path exists but drug->gene MOA not in DrugMechDB
label_definitions.unsupported_or_hub_artifact: no specific path / only promiscuous-hub bridge

rows.label: new_mechanism
rows.justification: Specific MOA path via ['SLC22A10'] exists, but the drug->target MOA is NOT in DrugMechDB (drug covered in DrugMechDB) and Probenecid is not an established oncology drug -- a non-obvious, hypothesis-generating mechanism.

rows.label: known_drug_new_cancer
rows.justification: Cisplatin is already an indicated oncology drug (e.g. ovarian cancer, hereditary breast ovarian cancer syndrome); a plausible MOA path exists for this new cancer via ['GSTT1'], but the specific drug->gene MOA is not in DrugMechDB.

rows.label: known_drug_new_cancer

rows.label: known_drug_new_cancer

rows.label: known_drug_new_cancer
rows.justification: Liothyronine is already an indicated oncology drug (e.g. goiter, multinodular 1, with or without Sertoli-Leydig cell tumors, familial thyroid carcinoma); a plausible MOA path exists for this new cancer via ['PCNA'], but the specific drug->gene MOA is not in DrugMechDB.

rows.label: new_mechanism
rows.justification: Specific MOA path via ['SLC12A6', 'OXSR1'] exists, but the drug->target MOA is NOT in DrugMechDB (drug absent in DrugMechDB) and Potassium chloride is not an established oncology drug -- a non-obvious, hypothesis-generating mechanism.
rows.best_path: Potassium chloride --targets--> SLC12A6 --interacts--> OXSR1 <--associated-- metastatic melanoma
